county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Kewaunee County formed 1852 from Door County. County seat is Kewaunee. Lake Michigan county with very strong Bohemian (Czech) and Belgian (Walloon) immigrant communities. One of the most concentrated Czech settlements in America.
county_metadata.research_tips: Kewaunee County Register of Deeds in Kewaunee holds land records from 1852. Lake Michigan county with very strong Bohemian (Czech) and Belgian (Walloon) immigrant communities -- one of the most concentrated Czech settlements in America. The Kewaunee County Historical Society maintains collections. WHS has microfilmed Kewaunee County records. Czech Catholic and Belgian Catholic parish records (Diocese of Green Bay) are essential for family research. Krok (Czech-language newspaper) published in Kewaunee. The Bohemian Hall and cultural organizations documented community life. FamilySearch has some Kewaunee County records indexed. BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Kewaunee County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Kewaunee; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Kewaunee County Deed Books -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.repository: Kewaunee County Register of Deeds, Kewaunee
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original deed books for Kewaunee County from 1852; grantor/grantee indexes; mortgages and liens; Register of Deeds office maintains land records. WHS has microfilm of historical deed books
source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1852; no major record losses

source_profiles.source_name: Kewaunee County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents (preemption, homestead, cash entry, military bounty) for Kewaunee County; Wisconsin is a public land state and GLO patents document original federal land transfers to private ownership

source_profiles.repository: Kewaunee County Probate Court, Kewaunee
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, inventories, guardianships, and estate settlements for Kewaunee County from 1852; Probate Court has jurisdiction over estate matters; WHS has microfilm of historical probate records

source_profiles.repository: Kewaunee County Clerk, Kewaunee / Wisconsin Historical Society, Madison
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage records for Kewaunee County from 1852; County Clerk issued marriage licenses in Wisconsin; WHS holds some county marriage records on microfilm; FamilySearch has indexed Wisconsin marriages; state vital records registration began 1907 (sporadic earlier compliance)

source_profiles.repository: Wisconsin Historical Society, Madison
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Historic newspapers for Kewaunee County towns at WHS on microfilm and digital; legal notices, obituaries, marriage announcements, estate settlements, and community news; WHS has one of the nation's largest newspaper collections

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: La Crosse County formed 1851 from Crawford (unorganized territory). County seat is La Crosse on the Mississippi River. Major river city with German and Norwegian settlement. Brewing industry heritage. University of Wisconsin-La Crosse.
county_metadata.research_tips: La Crosse County Register of Deeds in La Crosse holds land records from 1851. Major Mississippi River city with excellent archival resources. The La Crosse Public Library Archives has extensive local history and genealogy collections. Murphy Library Special Collections at University of Wisconsin-La Crosse hold regional materials. WHS has microfilmed La Crosse County records. Very strong German and Norwegian settlement. Brewing industry records (Heileman Brewing, City Brewery). La Crosse city directories from the 1860s. River commerce and steamboat records at WHS. Catholic and Lutheran parish records essential. FamilySearch has indexed La Crosse County records. Riverside Cemetery records date from the 1850s.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for La Crosse County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in La Crosse; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1851; no major record losses

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Lafayette County formed 1846 from Iowa County. County seat is Darlington. Southwestern Wisconsin lead mining county. Cornish, Welsh, and Irish miners followed by German and Norwegian farmers. First Capitol at Belmont (1836).
county_metadata.research_tips: Lafayette County Register of Deeds in Darlington holds land records from 1846. Created from Iowa County -- check Iowa County for pre-1846 records. Southwestern Wisconsin lead mining region. Cornish, Welsh, and Irish miners settled in the 1820s-1840s, followed by German and Norwegian farmers. The Lafayette County Historical Society in Darlington maintains collections. WHS has microfilmed Lafayette County records. Lead mining records at WHS document early settlers. First Capitol Historic Site in Belmont (temporary territorial capital 1836). FamilySearch has some Lafayette County records. BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries. Methodist and Episcopal church records from early mining communities.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Lafayette County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Darlington; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1846; no major record losses

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Langlade County formed 1879 from Oconto County (originally named New County, renamed 1880). County seat is Antigo. Northern Wisconsin lumber and farming county. German and Polish settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Langlade County Register of Deeds in Antigo holds land records from 1879. Originally named New County (renamed Langlade 1880). Created from Oconto County -- check Oconto County for pre-1879 records. Northern Wisconsin lumber and farming county. German and Polish immigrants were the primary settler groups. The Langlade County Historical Society in Antigo maintains local collections. WHS has microfilmed Langlade County records. Deleglise Cabin museum in Antigo. Lumber company records at WHS. Catholic and Lutheran parish records essential for German and Polish families. FamilySearch has some Langlade County records. BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Langlade County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Antigo; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1879; no major record losses

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Lincoln County formed 1874 from Marathon County. County seat is Merrill. Northern Wisconsin lumber county on the Wisconsin River. Strong German immigrant settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Lincoln County Register of Deeds in Merrill holds land records from 1874. Created from Marathon County -- check Marathon County for pre-1874 records. Major lumber industry county along the Wisconsin River. German immigrants were predominant. The Lincoln County Historical Society in Merrill maintains collections. WHS has microfilmed Lincoln County records. T.B. Scott Free Library in Merrill has local history. Lumber baron records at WHS. Jenny Bull Falls historic area. Catholic and Lutheran church records supplement civil records. FamilySearch has some Lincoln County records. BLM GLO patents.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Lincoln County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Merrill; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Manitowoc County formed 1836 from Brown County. County seat is Manitowoc on Lake Michigan. One of Wisconsin's original counties. Major shipbuilding center (WWII submarines). German, Norwegian, Czech, and Polish communities.
county_metadata.research_tips: Manitowoc County Register of Deeds in Manitowoc holds land records from 1836. One of Wisconsin's original counties on Lake Michigan. Major shipbuilding center (WWII submarine production at Manitowoc Shipbuilding Company). Very strong German, Norwegian, Bohemian (Czech), and Polish immigrant communities. The Manitowoc County Historical Society has excellent collections including the Wisconsin Maritime Museum shipyard records. WHS has microfilmed Manitowoc County records. The Pinecrest Historical Village. Catholic and Lutheran parish records (Archdiocese of Milwaukee, Diocese of Green Bay) are essential. Manitowoc city directories. FamilySearch has indexed many Manitowoc County records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Manitowoc County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Manitowoc; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Marathon County formed 1850 from Portage County. County seat is Wausau. Wisconsin's largest county by area. Major lumber industry center. Very strong German and Polish settlement. Paper mill industry.
county_metadata.research_tips: Marathon County Register of Deeds in Wausau holds land records from 1850. Wisconsin's largest county by area. Major lumber industry center on the Wisconsin River. Very strong German (Catholic and Lutheran) and Polish immigrant settlement -- Wausau was one of Wisconsin's most heavily German cities. The Marathon County Historical Society in Wausau has extensive collections. WHS has microfilmed Marathon County records. Leigh Yawkey Woodson Art Museum. Wausau city directories from the 1870s. Catholic diocese records (Diocese of La Crosse) and Lutheran synod records are critical. Paper mill and lumber company employment records. FamilySearch has indexed Marathon County records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Marathon County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Wausau; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Marinette County formed 1879 from Oconto County. County seat is Marinette on the Menominee River. Named for fur trader Queen Marinette. Lumber industry. Great Peshtigo Fire of 1871 devastated parts of the county.
county_metadata.research_tips: Marinette County Register of Deeds in Marinette holds land records from 1879. Created from Oconto County -- check Oconto County for pre-1879 records. Northeastern Wisconsin county on the Michigan border at the Menominee River. Named for fur trader Queen Marinette (Marie Antoinette Chevalier). Major lumber industry county. The Great Peshtigo Fire of October 8, 1871 devastated parts of Marinette County -- the deadliest wildfire in US history. Some early records were lost. The Marinette County Historical Museum and Peshtigo Fire Museum document the disaster. WHS has microfilmed Marinette County records. German, Scandinavian, and French-Canadian settlement. FamilySearch has some records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Marinette County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Marinette; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1879; no major record losses

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Marquette County formed 1836 from Brown County. County seat is Montello. One of Wisconsin's original counties in central Wisconsin. Named for Father Jacques Marquette. John Muir boyhood home area. Mixed settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Marquette County Register of Deeds in Montello holds land records from 1836. One of Wisconsin's original counties. Named for Father Jacques Marquette, the French Jesuit missionary. Central Wisconsin county with mixed Yankee, German, and Norwegian settlement. The Marquette County Historical Society in Westfield maintains collections. WHS has microfilmed Marquette County records. John Muir's boyhood home was near Montello (Muir Park). Church records (Methodist, Lutheran, Catholic) are important supplements. FamilySearch has some Marquette County records. BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries. Check Brown County for the earliest territorial-era records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Marquette County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Montello; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Marquette County Deed Books -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.repository: Marquette County Register of Deeds, Montello
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original deed books for Marquette County from 1836; grantor/grantee indexes; mortgages and liens; Register of Deeds office maintains land records. WHS has microfilm of historical deed books
source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1836; no major record losses

source_profiles.source_name: Marquette County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents (preemption, homestead, cash entry, military bounty) for Marquette County; Wisconsin is a public land state and GLO patents document original federal land transfers to private ownership

source_profiles.source_name: Marquette County Probate Court Records
source_profiles.repository: Marquette County Probate Court, Montello
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, inventories, guardianships, and estate settlements for Marquette County from 1836; Probate Court has jurisdiction over estate matters; WHS has microfilm of historical probate records

source_profiles.source_name: Marquette County Marriage Records -- County Clerk / WHS / FamilySearch
source_profiles.repository: Marquette County Clerk, Montello / Wisconsin Historical Society, Madison
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage records for Marquette County from 1836; County Clerk issued marriage licenses in Wisconsin; WHS holds some county marriage records on microfilm; FamilySearch has indexed Wisconsin marriages; state vital records registration began 1907 (sporadic earlier compliance)

source_profiles.source_name: Marquette County Federal and State Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry / WHS
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1840-1950 for Marquette County; Wisconsin state census 1855, 1865, 1875, 1885, 1895, 1905 at WHS (unusually detailed -- include place of birth, occupation, citizenship status); territorial census 1836 and 1842 may include Marquette County entries

source_profiles.source_name: Marquette County Historic Newspapers -- WHS
source_profiles.repository: Wisconsin Historical Society, Madison
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Historic newspapers for Marquette County towns at WHS on microfilm and digital; legal notices, obituaries, marriage announcements, estate settlements, and community news; WHS has one of the nation's largest newspaper collections

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Marquette County including church cemeteries, municipal cemeteries, and pioneer family burial grounds; Find A Grave has growing coverage; Wisconsin GenWeb and local historical societies have published transcriptions; DAR chapters have documented many Wisconsin cemeteries

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Menominee County formed 1961 from Menominee Indian Reservation (previously parts of Oconto and Shawano). County seat is Keshena. Wisconsin's newest county, coterminous with the Menominee Reservation. The entire county is tribal land.
county_metadata.research_tips: Menominee County Register of Deeds in Keshena holds land records from 1961. Wisconsin's newest county, created in 1961 from the Menominee Indian Reservation (previously part of Oconto and Shawano counties). The entire county is coterminous with the Menominee Reservation. Menominee tribal enrollment records, allotment records, and BIA records at NARA Great Lakes Region (Chicago) are essential. Pre-1961 records are in Oconto and Shawano counties. The Menominee Logging Camp Museum in Keshena documents the tribe's timber industry. WHS has some Menominee County records. College of Menominee Nation library has tribal history materials. FamilySearch has very limited Menominee County records due to its recent creation.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Menominee County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Keshena; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Menominee County Deed Books -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.repository: Menominee County Register of Deeds, Keshena
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original deed books for Menominee County from 1961; grantor/grantee indexes; mortgages and liens; Register of Deeds office maintains land records. WHS has microfilm of historical deed books
source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1961; no major record losses

source_profiles.repository: Menominee County Probate Court, Keshena
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, inventories, guardianships, and estate settlements for Menominee County from 1961; Probate Court has jurisdiction over estate matters; WHS has microfilm of historical probate records

source_profiles.repository: Menominee County Clerk, Keshena / Wisconsin Historical Society, Madison
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage records for Menominee County from 1961; County Clerk issued marriage licenses in Wisconsin; WHS holds some county marriage records on microfilm; FamilySearch has indexed Wisconsin marriages; state vital records registration began 1907 (sporadic earlier compliance)

source_profiles.source_name: Menominee County Federal Census Records (post-1961)
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1970 for Menominee County (formed 1961); for earlier census data, search Oconto and Shawano counties and the Menominee Indian Reservation census rolls at NARA. BIA Indian census rolls (1885-1940) enumerate Menominee tribal members on the reservation

source_profiles.source_name: Menominee Indian Tribe of Wisconsin Records -- Menominee County Area
source_profiles.repository: NARA Great Lakes Region (Chicago) / Bureau of Indian Affairs
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tribal enrollment records, allotment records, annuity rolls, census rolls, and BIA agency records for the Menominee Indian Tribe of Wisconsin in Menominee County; treaty records, removal records, and reservation establishment documents at NARA Great Lakes Region (Chicago). WHS also holds some Native American records
